title: Understanding the Problem
text: To find the time it takes for the rocket to reach a velocity of $$120$$ $$\frac{km}{h}$$, we need to use Newton's second law of motion. This involves calculating the acceleration and then using it to find the time.

title: Convert Velocity Units
text: First, convert the velocity from $$\frac{km}{h}$$ to $$\frac{m}{s}$$. Remember that $$1$$ $$\frac{km}{h}$$ is equal to $$\frac{1}{3.6}$$ $$\frac{m}{s}$$.

title: Calculate Acceleration
text: Use the formula for acceleration: \( $$a=\frac{F}{m}$$ \), where \( F \) is the thrust and \( $$m$$ \) is the mass of the rocket.

title: Use the Acceleration to Find Time
text: Now that you have the acceleration, use the formula \( $$v=at$$ \) to find the time \( $$t$$ \).

title: Calculate Time
text: Rearrange the formula \( $$v=at$$ \) to solve for \( $$t$$ \): \( $$t=\frac{v}{a}$$ \). Use the converted velocity and the calculated acceleration.
